title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: Experimental design foundational component plant breeding agronomic research. ability accurately estimate genetic effects, compare treatments, predict breeding values depends critically field trials constructed. Classical designs — randomised complete block designs (RCBD), alpha-lattice designs, augmented designs — provide well-understood statistical properties make assumptions modern breeding programs increasingly meet: regular block structures, balanced replication across entries, prior knowledge genetic relationships. Contemporary trials involve large numbers candidates, limited field capacity, spatial heterogeneity, rich genomic information classical frameworks treat external design process. OptiDesign addresses challenges unified framework integrates: flexible field layout construction two design families genetic structure family labels, pedigree matrices, genomic relationship matrices optional spatial dispersion optimisation reduce clustering related entries mixed-model efficiency evaluation , D, CDmean optimality criteria criterion-driven design search returns statistically best design across many randomisations package follows single-responsibility architecture: construction, evaluation, optimisation separated distinct functions can called independently chained, making step transparent reproducible.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: -criterion (lower better): minimises mean pairwise contrast variance fixed treatment effects, mean prediction error variance (PEV) random treatment effects. Acriterion=2p(p−1)∑<jVar(τ̂−τ̂j)A_\text{criterion} = \frac{2}{p(p-1)} \sum_{<j} \text{Var}(\hat{\tau}_i - \hat{\tau}_j) D-criterion (lower better): minimises geometric mean contrast covariance eigenvalues (fixed effects ). Dcriterion=exp(logdet(HVH)p−1)D_\text{criterion} = \exp\!\left(\frac{\log\det(HVH)}{p-1}\right) H=Ip−p−1JpH = I_p - p^{-1}J_p centering matrix VV treatment variance-covariance submatrix C−1C^{-1}. CDmean (higher better): mean coefficient determination genomic breeding value (GEBV) prediction (Rincent et al. 2012). Measures proportion genetic variance explained prediction average across lines. CDmean=1−mean PEVσg2\text{CDmean} = 1 - \frac{\text{mean PEV}}{\sigma_g^2} CDmean ranges 0 (information) 1 (perfect prediction). primary criterion optimising training population designs genomic selection.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: prep_famoptg() builds repeated-check block design checks appear every block non-check entries allocated across blocks according specified replication levels. Three design classes share function: Augmented design — test entries unreplicated, checks repeated every block. Suitable large entry sets resources allow single observation per candidate. P-rep design — entries replicated across multiple distinct blocks, others unreplicated. general case: mixture candidate entries different priority levels. RCBD-type design — non-check entries equally replicated. replication number equals n_blocks, every entry appears every block — closest repeated-check analogue classical RCBD. p-rep constraint — core structural rule: replicated treatment ever appears twice block. Enforced construction every call, post-hoc checking. Total required plots: total=nblocks×nchecks+∑=1vpri+vu\text{total} = n_\text{blocks} \times n_\text{checks} + \sum_{=1}^{v_p} r_i + v_u rir_i replication count p-rep entry ii vuv_u number unreplicated entries.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: alpha_rc_stream() builds fixed-grid alpha row-column design using stream-based layout. field converted one-dimensional planting stream, partitioned nrepsn_\text{reps} contiguous replicate segments, segment divided incomplete blocks. Checks appear every incomplete block; entry appears exactly per replicate. Unused cells appear end stream. Block-size constraints expressed total block size (checks + entries) via min_block_size max_block_size. number incomplete blocks per replicate bb must satisfy: ⌈vmax_block_size−c⌉≤b≤⌊vmin_block_size−c⌋\left\lceil \frac{v}{\text{max\_block\_size} - c} \right\rceil \leq b \leq \left\lfloor \frac{v}{\text{min\_block\_size} - c} \right\rfloor vv number entries cc number checks.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: Wraps alpha_rc_stream() evaluate_alpha_efficiency() optimisation loop three search strategies: RS (Random Restart) — generate n_restarts independent designs, return best. Guaranteed validity, simple, easily parallelisable. SA (Simulated Annealing) — iterative entry permutation swaps Metropolis acceptance: P(accept worse)=exp(−ΔTk)P(\text{accept worse}) = \exp\!\left(-\frac{\Delta}{T_k}\right) TkT_k cools sa_temp_start sa_temp_end. Better escaping local optima RS. Invalid swap proposals treated neutral events affect acceptance rate. GA (Genetic Algorithm) — population entry permutations evolved via Order Crossover (OX1), random swap mutation, tournament selection, elitism. powerful global search.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: optimisers implement four-point integrity checking strategy guarantees returned design structurally valid: Post-construction — every candidate validated immediately prep_famoptg() alpha_rc_stream() returns, scoring. Pre-storage — candidate re-checked updating running best. Pre-return — stored best re-checked one final time returning user. Emergency fallback — valid design found iterations, 10 fresh random designs attempted stopping informative error. prep_famoptg family, five structural constraints verified: non-check entry appears single block p-rep treatment appears exactly p_rep_reps[] blocks unreplicated treatment appears exactly checks appear every block p-rep treatment occupies block twice (core p-rep guarantee)

what: Workflow 4: CDmean Optimisation for Genomic Selection
title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding
text: CDmean primary criterion objective maximising GEBV prediction reliability rather contrast precision. requires random treatment effects genomic prediction model. CDmean -criterion can point different directions measure different objectives — contrast precision versus prediction reliability. Choose based trial objective.
code: # CDmean optimisation with GBLUP requires a K matrix. # Here we use the example K from the shipped dataset. opt_cdmean <- optimize_alpha_rc(   check_treatments   = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$check_treatments,   check_families     = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$check_families,   entry_treatments   = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$entry_treatments,   entry_families     = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$entry_families,   n_reps             = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$n_reps,   n_rows             = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$n_rows,   n_cols             = x$OptiDesign_alpha_example$n_cols,   min_block_size     = 10L,   max_block_size     = 12L,   # Genomic prediction model   treatment_effect   = "random",   prediction_type    = "GBLUP",   K                  = x$OptiDesign_K,   line_id_map        = x$OptiDesign_id_map,   varcomp = list(     sigma_g2   = 0.4,     sigma_e2   = 0.6,     sigma_rep2 = 0.1,     sigma_ib2  = 0.05,     sigma_r2   = 0.02,     sigma_c2   = 0.02   ),   # CDmean criterion   method      = "RS",   criterion   = "CDmean",   n_restarts  = 20,   verbose_opt = FALSE )  cat("Best CDmean:", round(opt_cdmean$optimization$best_score, 4),     "(higher is better)\n") cat("CDmean from efficiency slot:",     round(opt_cdmean$efficiency$CDmean, 4), "\n")

title: OptiDesign
text: OptiDesign provides tools constructing, evaluating, optimizing experimental field designs plant breeding related agricultural applications. integrates: field layout construction genetic structure (family, pedigree, genomic relationships) optional spatial dispersion optimization statistical efficiency evaluation mixed models criterion-driven design optimization single, flexible workflow built around two design families.

title: OptiDesign
text: many breeding programs, experimental design treated purely logistical step. However, design choices strongly affect: precision treatment estimates genomic prediction accuracy ability separate genetic environmental effects robustness field spatial heterogeneity OptiDesign allows users explicitly control aspects design stage, rather correcting post hoc analysis. Uniquely, OptiDesign goes beyond single-design construction offering criterion-driven optimisation — searching across many randomisations return design best statistical properties specific trial objective.

title: OptiDesign
text: OptiDesign built around three key ideas.

title: OptiDesign
text: Designs reflect trials actually implemented field: fixed grid layouts (n_rows × n_cols) field-book ordering serpentine movement contiguous replicate structure unused cells placed end field stream

title: OptiDesign
text: Entries can arranged based known genetic structure: family labels pedigree relationships (matrix) genomic relationships (GRM) enables reduction local genetic relatedness, improved sampling genetic diversity across spatial blocks, better estimation genetic effects.

title: OptiDesign
text: Designs can evaluated optimised using mixed-model principles field implementation: fixed-effect precision (BLUEs) — D optimality criteria random-effect prediction (BLUP / GBLUP / PBLUP) — mean PEV CDmean spatial residual structures (IID, AR1, AR1×AR1) criterion-driven search returning statistically best design

title: OptiDesign
text: OptiDesign follows single-responsibility architecture: construction, evaluation, optimisation separated distinct functions can called independently chained together.

title: OptiDesign
text: Constructs repeated-check block designs flexible replication, covering augmented designs, partially replicated (p-rep) designs, RCBD-type repeated-check designs. Checks appear every block; entries may replicated, partially replicated, unreplicated. Use function : many entries limited field resources checks must appear every block need flexible framework covering augmented, p-rep, balanced layouts working early- intermediate-stage breeding trials Key capabilities: Key design rule: treatment can appear multiple times overall, always distinct blocks — never twice block.

title: OptiDesign
text: Evaluates statistical efficiency design produced prep_famoptg(). mixed model contains Block + Row + Column random effects (replicate incomplete-block nesting). Uses variance component sigma_b2 flat block structure. Fully decoupled construction — field book can evaluated multiple times different model assumptions without rebuilding layout. Supported criteria:

text: Wraps prep_famoptg() evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() Random Restart (RS) loop. RS used exclusively p-rep constraint enforced construction every call — permutation-based methods require block-aware swap logic preserve . Every candidate design valid construction. Supports , D, , CDmean criteria.

title: OptiDesign
code: library(OptiDesign)  # 1. Construct design <- prep_famoptg(   check_treatments        = checks,   check_families          = check_fam,   p_rep_treatments        = prep_trts,   p_rep_reps              = rep(2L, length(prep_trts)),   p_rep_families          = prep_fam,   unreplicated_treatments = unrep_trts,   unreplicated_families   = unrep_fam,   n_blocks = 5, n_rows = 15, n_cols = 20 )  # 2. Evaluate eff <- evaluate_famoptg_efficiency(   field_book         = design$field_book,   n_rows             = 15, n_cols = 20,   check_treatments   = checks,   treatment_effect   = "fixed",   residual_structure = "AR1xAR1",   rho_row = 0.10, rho_col = 0.10 ) eff$A_criterion   # lower is better eff$D_criterion  # 3. Or optimise directly (returns best of 50 random designs) opt <- optimize_famoptg(   check_treatments        = checks,   check_families          = check_fam,   p_rep_treatments        = prep_trts,   p_rep_reps              = rep(2L, length(prep_trts)),   p_rep_families          = prep_fam,   unreplicated_treatments = unrep_trts,   unreplicated_families   = unrep_fam,   n_blocks           = 5, n_rows = 15, n_cols = 20,   treatment_effect   = "fixed",   residual_structure = "AR1xAR1",   rho_row = 0.10, rho_col = 0.10,   criterion = "A", n_restarts = 50 ) opt$optimization$best_score opt$optimization$score_history

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding — OptiDesign-package
text: package provides two main design construction functions: prep_famoptg() constructs repeated-check block designs flexible replication, supporting three design classes: augmented repeated-check designs (checks repeated, entries unreplicated), partially replicated (p-rep) repeated-check designs (mixture replicated unreplicated entries), RCBD-type repeated-check designs (entries equally replicated). p-rep constraint - replicated entry appears twice block - enforced construction. Supports optional grouping family labels relationship matrices optional genetic dispersion optimisation. Construction ; evaluation performed separately evaluate_famoptg_efficiency(). alpha_rc_stream() constructs fixed-grid alpha row-column stream designs field treated global traversal stream. Replicates contiguous stream segments, incomplete blocks may unequal size, checks included every block, unused cells appear end stream. Construction ; evaluation performed separately evaluate_alpha_efficiency().

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding — OptiDesign-package
text: evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() evaluates statistical efficiency design produced prep_famoptg(). mixed model contains Block + Row Column random effects (replicate incomplete-block nesting). Variance component sigma_b2 controls block variance. Supported criteria: -criterion: mean pairwise contrast variance (fixed) mean PEV (random). Lower better. D-criterion: geometric mean contrast covariance eigenvalues (fixed ). Lower better. CDmean: mean coefficient determination GEBV prediction (Rincent et al. 2012). Higher better. Requires random treatment effects genomic prediction model. evaluate_alpha_efficiency() evaluates statistical efficiency design produced alpha_rc_stream(). mixed model contains Rep + IBlock(Rep) + Row + Column random effects. Variance components sigma_rep2 sigma_ib2 control replicate incomplete-block variance respectively. Supports , D, CDmean criteria evaluate_famoptg_efficiency(). evaluation functions fully decoupled construction field book can evaluated multiple times different model assumptions without rebuilding layout.

title: OptiDesign: Optimized Experimental Field Design for Plant Breeding — OptiDesign-package
text: optimize_famoptg() wraps prep_famoptg() evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() Random Restart (RS) optimisation loop. RS used exclusively p-rep constraint (replicated treatment block twice) enforced construction every prep_famoptg() call - permutation-based methods SA GA require block-aware swap logic preserve constraint. Every candidate valid design constraints satisfied. Supports , D, , CDmean criteria. optimize_alpha_rc() wraps alpha_rc_stream() evaluate_alpha_efficiency() optimisation loop three search strategies: RS (Random Restart): generate many independent designs, return best criterion. Simple fully safe construction. SA (Simulated Annealing): iteratively propose entry permutation swaps, accepting improvements always degradations temperature-governed probability. Repeated across multiple restarts. GA (Genetic Algorithm): maintain population entry permutations evolve using Order Crossover (OX1), swap mutation, elitism. methods optimisers preserve structural constraints construction. implement four-point integrity checking strategy: post-construction validation, re-check storing best, final check return, emergency fallback valid design found.

what: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: alpha_rc_stream() builds randomised alpha-lattice incomplete block design breeding agronomic experiments fixed field grid n_rows x n_cols plots. field converted one-dimensional planting stream according order serpentine, partitioned n_reps contiguous replicate segments, replicate divided incomplete blocks. Every incomplete block contains check treatments plus subset entry treatments. Entry treatments appear exactly per replicate. Trailing unused field cells remain NA layout matrix field book. Design evaluation separated construction. function returns field book, layout matrix, design metadata . compute -, D-, CDmean optimality criteria, call evaluate_alpha_efficiency() returned field_book. search optimised design using Random Restart, Simulated Annealing, Genetic Algorithm, call optimize_alpha_rc().

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: check_treatments Character vector check treatment identifiers. Checks appear every incomplete block every replicate. Must overlap entry_treatments must contain duplicates. check_families Character vector length check_treatments. Family labels checks, used adjacency scoring. Always stored Family column; Gcluster always NA check plots. entry_treatments Character vector non-check treatment identifiers. entry appears exactly per replicate. Must contain duplicates. entry_families Character vector length entry_treatments. Family labels entries. Used adjacency scoring cluster_source = "Family" set number clusters cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""). n_reps Positive integer. Number contiguous replicate segments. n_rows Positive integer. Number field rows. n_cols Positive integer. Number field columns. order Character. Stream traversal direction: "row" fills row row; "column" fills column column. serpentine Logical. TRUE, alternate rows (order = "row") alternate columns (order = "column") traverse reverse direction. seed Optional integer. NULL, seed drawn 1:.Machine$integer.max returned seed_used. Controls randomised steps including entry randomisation, within-block shuffling, check placement, dispersion optimisation. attempts Positive integer. Number within-block shuffling attempts minimise adjacent -group pairs. Exits early score reaches zero. warn_and_correct Logical. Retained interface compatibility. Field capacity violations always raise error. fix_rows Logical. Retained interface compatibility. Field geometry always fixed. cluster_source Character. Grouping source adjacency scoring. "Family" uses entry_families directly. "GRM" "" derive clusters PCA respective relationship matrix. GRM Optional square numeric matrix rownames colnames equal line IDs. Required cluster_source = "GRM" use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "GRM". Optional square numeric matrix. Required cluster_source = "" dispersion_source = "". id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match relationship matrix rownames. cluster_method Character. Clustering algorithm: "kmeans" uses stats::kmeans() cluster_attempts random starts; "hclust" uses Ward's D2 linkage. cluster_seed Integer. Seed k-means initialisation, run isolated RNG scope. cluster_attempts Positive integer. Number k-means random restarts. Ignored cluster_method = "hclust". n_pcs_use Positive number Inf. Number leading PCs matrix-based clustering. Actual number used min(n_pcs_use, n_positive_eigenvalues - 1). n_blocks_per_rep Optional positive integer. supplied, exact number incomplete blocks per replicate. Validated feasible range derived min_block_size, max_block_size, field capacity. NULL, block count set automatically \(b_\text{max}\). min_block_size Optional positive integer \(\geq c\). Minimum total block size (checks + entries). Sets upper bound \(b\). max_block_size Optional positive integer \(\geq c\). Maximum total block size (checks + entries). Sets lower bound \(b\). check_placement Character. Check position rule within blocks. "systematic" distributes checks evenly; "random" draws positions uniformly without replacement. check_position_pattern Character. Retained interface compatibility. use_dispersion Logical. TRUE, apply post-layout swap-based local dispersion optimisation reduce pairwise relatedness among neighbouring non-check plots. dispersion_source Character. Matrix used dispersion scoring: "K", "", "GRM". corresponding argument must non-NULL. dispersion_radius Positive integer. Chebyshev distance radius defining neighbourhood dispersion scoring. dispersion_iters Non-negative integer. Number random swap proposals dispersion optimisation. dispersion_seed Integer. Seed dispersion step, run isolated RNG scope. Defaults seed_used NULL. K Optional square numeric matrix. Used dispersion scoring dispersion_source = "K". line_id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match rownames(K) dispersion matrix. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints one-line summary field usage, trailing NA plots, replicate sizes, block count origin, block sizes replicate 1, active block-size bounds.

Columns: Plot (stream position), Row, Column, Rep (NA unused), IBlock (global block index), BlockInRep (block index within rep), Treatment (NA unused), Family, Gcluster (NA cluster_source = "Family" check plots), Check (logical). design_info Named list: n_rows, n_cols, total_plots, n_reps, n_checks, n_entries, n_blocks_per_rep (resolved), n_blocks_per_rep_user, min_block_size, max_block_size, min_entry_slots_per_block, max_entry_slots_per_block, rep_sizes, total_used_plots, trailing_na_plots, block_plan, block_meta, order, serpentine. seed_used Integer. random seed used.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: Let: \(r\) = number replicates (n_reps) \(c\) = number checks (length(check_treatments)) \(v\) = number entries (length(entry_treatments)) \(b\) = number incomplete blocks per replicate replicate contains \(v\) entries exactly \(c\) checks repeated \(b\) incomplete blocks. Entries split across blocks evenly possible - block entry counts differ one. number used plots per replicate : $$\text{plots per rep} = v + b \times c$$ total used plots design : $$\text{total used} = r \times (v + b \times c)$$ fixed field contains cells total, unused trailing cells remain NA. function stops required plots exceed field capacity.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: Block-size constraints expressed terms total block size (checks + entries) repeated-check design whole block operational unit. mapping : $$\text{min\_entry\_slots} = \text{min\_block\_size} - c$$ $$\text{max\_entry\_slots} = \text{max\_block\_size} - c$$ example, \(c = 3\) checks, min_block_size = 19 max_block_size = 20 means block holds 16 17 entries plus 3 check positions. bounds must \(\geq c\).

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: translating total-block-size limits entry-slot limits, number incomplete blocks per replicate \(b\) must satisfy feasible range \([b_\text{min},\, b_\text{max}]\): Lower bound max_block_size \(b \geq \lceil v / (\text{max\_block\_size} - c)\rceil\) Upper bound min_block_size \(b \leq \lfloor v / (\text{min\_block\_size} - c)\rfloor\) Upper bound field capacity \(b \leq \lfloor (\text{total\_plots}/r - v) / c \rfloor\) function stops informative error \(b_\text{min} > b_\text{max}\). least one n_blocks_per_rep, min_block_size, max_block_size must supplied. User-fixed mode (n_blocks_per_rep supplied): value validated feasible range block-size constraints. Automatic mode (n_blocks_per_rep = NULL): \(b\) set \(b_\text{max}\) - largest feasible number blocks, producing smallest feasible blocks, generally gives best local control.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: field mapped one-dimensional stream iterating rows (order = "row") columns (order = "column"). serpentine = TRUE, alternate rows columns reverse direction, producing boustrophedon planting path. Replicate segments occupy contiguous portions stream incomplete blocks occupy contiguous sub-segments within replicate.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: Entries randomised independently within replicate, split across blocks. Checks inserted positions determined check_placement: "systematic": positions computed round(seq(1, block_size, length.= n_checks + 2))[2:(n_checks + 1)], distributing checks evenly possible. "random": positions drawn uniformly without replacement. Within-block order optimised attempts random shuffles minimise count adjacent -group treatment pairs. search exits early adjacency score reaches zero.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: grouping label used adjacency scoring determined cluster_source: "Family": uses entry_families check_families directly. Gcluster NA plots. "GRM" "": derives clusters leading principal components relationship matrix. number clusters equals length(unique(entry_families)). Cluster labels prefixed "G" (GRM) "" (matrix). Check treatments always use check_families always Gcluster = NA.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — alpha_rc_stream
text: use_dispersion = TRUE, local swap search applied layout construction. dispersion_iters iterations, two non-check plots selected random; swap accepted reduces total neighbourhood relatedness score - sum relationship matrix values non-check neighbour pairs within Chebyshev distance dispersion_radius. matrix used selected dispersion_source.

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: field_book Data frame produced alpha_rc_stream(). Must contain columns: Plot, Row, Column, Rep, IBlock, BlockInRep, Treatment, Check. n_rows Positive integer. Number field rows (must match original design). n_cols Positive integer. Number field columns (must match original design). check_treatments Character vector check treatment identifiers (must match used alpha_rc_stream()). treatment_effect Character. Whether entry treatments modelled "fixed" (BLUE-based D criteria) "random" (PEV-based criterion CDmean). Note: "fixed" produce CDmean; "random" produce D-criterion. prediction_type Character. Random-effect prediction model entries. "IID" assumes independent entries (\(G^{-1} = \sigma_g^{-2} \)). "GBLUP" "PBLUP" use supplied K matrix (\(G^{-1} = \sigma_g^{-2} K^{-1}\)). "none" invalid treatment_effect = "random" raises error. check_as_fixed Logical. TRUE, checks included fixed effects model. residual_structure Character. Residual covariance structure. "IID" (independence), "AR1" (row AR1 ), "AR1xAR1" (separable row x column AR1). rho_row Numeric \((-1, 1)\). AR1 autocorrelation parameter along rows. Used residual_structure %% c("AR1", "AR1xAR1"). rho_col Numeric \((-1, 1)\). AR1 autocorrelation parameter along columns. Used residual_structure = "AR1xAR1". varcomp Named list variance components. Required components: sigma_e2 (residual), sigma_g2 (entry genetic), sigma_rep2 (replicate), sigma_ib2 (incomplete block), sigma_r2 (row), sigma_c2 (column). components must present even corresponding effect absent model. sigma_g2 used denominator CDmean treatment_effect = "random". K Optional square numeric matrix rownames colnames. Required prediction_type %% c("GBLUP", "PBLUP"). line_id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match rownames(K). spatial_engine Character. Computational engine matrix operations. "auto" uses "dense" used plots \(\leq\) dense_max_n, otherwise "sparse". dense_max_n Integer. Threshold spatial_engine = "auto". eff_trace_samples Positive integer. Number Rademacher vectors Hutchinson stochastic trace estimator (large designs ). eff_full_max Positive integer. Maximum number treatments exact \(C^{-1}\) submatrix extraction. Designs treatments use stochastic estimator (mode suffix _APPROX).

text: named list containing model metadata criterion values: model Character. Model string. treatment_effect Character. supplied. prediction_type Character NA. residual_structure_requested Character. supplied. residual_structure_used Character. resolved. spatial_engine_used Character. "dense" "sparse". mode Character. Computation mode: "FIXED_TREATMENT_BLUE_CONTRAST", "FIXED_TREATMENT_BLUE_APPROX", "RANDOM_TREATMENT_PEV", "RANDOM_TREATMENT_PEV_APPROX". n_trt Integer. Number treatment columns evaluated. n_contrasts Integer. n_trt - 1. Fixed mode . A_criterion Numeric. Mean pairwise contrast variance (fixed) mean PEV (random). Lower better. D_criterion Numeric NA. Geometric mean contrast covariance eigenvalues. Fixed full mode . Lower better. A_efficiency Numeric. 1 / A_criterion. Higher better. D_efficiency Numeric NA. 1 / D_criterion. Higher better. Numeric. Alias A_efficiency (backward compatibility). D Numeric NA. Alias D_efficiency (backward compatibility). mean_VarDiff Numeric. Mean pairwise contrast variance. Fixed full mode . PEV_criterion Numeric. Mean PEV. Random mode . mean_PEV Numeric. Alias PEV_criterion. Random mode . CDmean Numeric. Mean coefficient determination GEBV prediction: \(1 - \text{mean\_PEV} / \sigma_g^2\). Range \([0,1]\). Higher better. Random mode . CD_per_line Numeric vector NA. Per-line coefficient determination: \(1 - \text{PEV}_i / \sigma_g^2\). Available full mode (NA _APPROX mode). Random mode .

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: model : $$y = X\beta + Zu + e$$ Fixed part \(X\): intercept, optional check fixed effects (check_as_fixed = TRUE), entry treatment fixed effects treatment_effect = "fixed". Random part \(Z\): replicate, incomplete block within replicate, row, column, - treatment_effect = "random" - entry treatment effects. Residual structure \(R = \sigma_e^2 \Sigma\): "IID": \(\Sigma = \) "AR1": row-AR1, \(Q_\text{AR1}(\rho_\text{row}) \otimes I_\text{cols}\) "AR1xAR1": separable row x column AR1, \(Q_\text{AR1}(\rho_\text{col}) \otimes Q_\text{AR1}(\rho_\text{row})\) AR1 precision matrices tridiagonal sparse matrices. \(n\)-dimensional AR1 process parameter \(\rho\), let \(= 1/(1-\rho^2)\): Interior diagonal: \((1+\rho^2) \times \) Edge diagonal (positions 1 \(n\)): \(\) -diagonal: \(-\rho \times \)

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: $$C = \begin{pmatrix} X^\top Q X & X^\top Q Z \\ Z^\top Q X &   Z^\top Q Z + G^{-1} \end{pmatrix}$$ \(Q = R^{-1}\) residual precision matrix.

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: "IID": \(G^{-1}_\text{entry} = \sigma_g^{-2} \) "GBLUP" / "PBLUP": \(G^{-1}_\text{entry} = \sigma_g^{-2} K^{-1}\), computed via sparse Cholesky; falls back Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse near-singular K.

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: Metrics computed submatrix \(C^{-1}\) corresponding treatment columns. -criterion (lower = better): $$A_\text{criterion} = \bar{v}_\text{diff} =   \frac{2}{p(p-1)} \sum_{<j} \text{Var}(\hat{\tau}_i - \hat{\tau}_j)$$ D-criterion (lower = better): $$D_\text{criterion} = \exp\!\left(\frac{\log\det(HVH)}{p-1}\right)$$ \(H = I_p - p^{-1}J_p\) centering matrix \(V\) treatment variance-covariance submatrix. Efficiency forms (higher = better): $$A_\text{efficiency} = 1 / A_\text{criterion}, \quad       D_\text{efficiency} = 1 / D_\text{criterion}$$

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: PEV-criterion (lower = better): mean prediction error variance across entry lines. $$\text{PEV}_\text{criterion} = \frac{1}{p}\sum_{=1}^{p}   \text{Var}(\hat{u}_i - u_i)$$ CDmean (higher = better): mean coefficient determination genomic breeding value prediction (Rincent et al. 2012, Genetics 192:715-728). $$\text{CDmean} = 1 - \frac{\text{PEV}_\text{criterion}}{\sigma_g^2}$$ CDmean measures proportion genetic variance explained GEBV predictions average across lines. Values close 1 indicate highly reliable predictions; values close 0 indicate near-uninformative predictions. CDmean defined treatment_effect = "random". CD per line: number treatments exceed eff_full_max, per-line coefficient determination vector also returned: $$CD_i = 1 - \text{PEV}_i / \sigma_g^2$$

title: Evaluate the statistical efficiency of an alpha-lattice design — evaluate_alpha_efficiency
text: number treatments exceeds eff_full_max, Hutchinson stochastic trace estimator eff_trace_samples Rademacher vectors replaces exact submatrix extraction. mode field carries suffix _APPROX D_criterion, D_efficiency, CD_per_line NA.

what: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: prep_famoptg() builds repeated-check block design plant breeding agronomic experiments. Check treatments included every block, non-check treatments allocated according user-specified replication levels. Depending treatment structure supplied, function generates three design classes: Augmented repeated-check design: checks repeated every block, test entries unreplicated (p_rep_treatments = NULL). Partially replicated (p-rep) repeated-check design: mixture replicated unreplicated non-check entries. RCBD-type repeated-check design: non-check entries given replication across blocks. Design evaluation separated construction. function returns field book, layout matrix, seed . compute , D, CDmean optimality criteria call evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() returned field_book. search optimised design across many randomisations call optimize_famoptg().

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: check_treatments Character vector check treatment identifiers. Checks appear every block. Must overlap p_rep_treatments unreplicated_treatments must contain duplicates. check_families Character vector length check_treatments. Family labels checks, used adjacency scoring. Always stored Family column; Gcluster always NA check plots. p_rep_treatments Character vector replicated non-check treatment identifiers. entry appears exactly p_rep_reps[] distinct blocks. May NULL character(0) augmented designs. p_rep_reps Integer vector length p_rep_treatments. Number blocks p-rep treatment appears . Must satisfy 1 <= p_rep_reps[] <= n_blocks \(\). p_rep_families Character vector length p_rep_treatments. Family labels p-rep entries. unreplicated_treatments Character vector treatment identifiers appear exactly full design. May NULL character(0). unreplicated_families Character vector length unreplicated_treatments. Family labels unreplicated entries. n_blocks Positive integer. Number experimental blocks. n_rows Positive integer. Number field rows. n_cols Positive integer. Number field columns. order Character. Field traversal direction: "column" fills column column; "row" fills row row. serpentine Logical. TRUE, alternate columns (order = "column") alternate rows (order = "row") traverse reverse direction, producing boustrophedon planting path. seed Optional integer. NULL, seed drawn 1:.Machine$integer.max returned seed_used. Controls randomised steps: p-rep block assignment, unreplicated entry distribution, within-block shuffling, check placement, dispersion optimisation. attempts Positive integer. Number within-block shuffle attempts minimise adjacent -group pairs. Default 1000. warn_and_correct Logical. TRUE, silently adjust field dimensions n_rows * n_cols match required total plots. FALSE, stop error. fix_rows Logical. Used warn_and_correct = TRUE. TRUE, hold n_rows fixed expand n_cols; otherwise hold n_cols fixed expand n_rows. cluster_source Character. Grouping source adjacency scoring: "Family", "GRM", "". GRM Optional square numeric matrix rownames colnames equal line IDs. Required cluster_source = "GRM" dispersion_source = "GRM". Optional square numeric matrix. Required cluster_source = "" dispersion_source = "". id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match relationship matrix rownames. cluster_method Character. Clustering algorithm: "kmeans" "hclust" (Ward's D2 linkage). cluster_seed Integer. Seed k-means initialisation, run isolated RNG scope. cluster_attempts Positive integer. Number k-means random restarts (nstart). n_pcs_use Positive number Inf. Leading PCs retained matrix-based clustering. Actual number used min(n_pcs_use, n_positive_eigenvalues - 1). check_placement Character. Check position rule within blocks: "random" Check positions drawn uniformly. "systematic" Check positions spaced evenly block. "optimal" Keeps layout maximises mean nearest-neighbour distance check plots field across check_opt_attempts candidates. check_opt_attempts Positive integer. Number candidate layouts evaluated check_placement = "optimal". Default 200. use_dispersion Logical. TRUE, apply post-layout swap-based dispersion optimisation reduce pairwise relatedness among neighbouring non-check plots. dispersion_source Character. Matrix used dispersion scoring: "K", "", "GRM". corresponding argument must non-NULL. dispersion_radius Positive integer. Chebyshev distance radius defining neighbourhood dispersion scoring. Default 1. dispersion_iters Non-negative integer. Number swap proposals dispersion optimisation. Default 2000. dispersion_seed Integer. Seed dispersion step, run isolated RNG scope. K Optional square numeric matrix. Used dispersion scoring dispersion_source = "K". line_id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match rownames(K) dispersion matrix. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints one-line summary field dimensions, block count, treatment counts.

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: named list three components: layout_matrix Character matrix dimension n_rows x n_cols. Used cells contain treatment IDs; unused cells (field larger required) NA. field_book Data frame one row per assigned plot. Columns: Treatment, Family (original user-supplied label), Gcluster (NA cluster_source = "Family" check plots; otherwise matrix-derived cluster label prefixed "G" ""), Block (integer 1 n_blocks), Plot (sequential integer), Row, Column. seed_used Integer. random seed used.

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: Let: \(c\) = number check treatments \(b\) = n_blocks \(v_p\) = number p-rep treatments replication counts \(r_1, r_2, \ldots, r_{v_p}\) \(v_u\) = number unreplicated treatments Total plots required: $$\text{total} = b \times c + \sum_{=1}^{v_p} r_i + v_u$$ Allocation rules enforced construction: Checks Appear every block. check contributes \(b\) plots total. P-rep treatments treatment \(\) appears exactly \(r_i\) blocks, always distinct blocks - never twice block. core p-rep constraint. Unreplicated treatments Appear exactly full design, distributed evenly possible across blocks.

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: Augmented repeated-check design - obtained p_rep_treatments empty non-check entries supplied via unreplicated_treatments. Checks repeated treatments. P-rep repeated-check design - obtained non-check entries p_rep_treatments p_rep_reps > 1 others unreplicated. general use case: mixture replicated candidate entries single-plot entries alongside repeated checks. RCBD-type repeated-check design - obtained non-check treatments supplied via p_rep_treatments common replication count greater 1. count equals n_blocks, every non-check treatment appears every block - closest repeated-check analogue classical RCBD.

Surplus cells remain NA layout matrix.

Block-size constraints expressed total block size (checks + entries) via min_block_size max_block_size.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: Added evaluate_famoptg_efficiency(): standalone mixed-model efficiency evaluator designs produced prep_famoptg(). Model contains Block + Row + Column random effects (sigma_b2). Computes -criterion, D-criterion, CDmean. Fully decoupled construction. Added evaluate_alpha_efficiency(): standalone mixed-model efficiency evaluator designs produced alpha_rc_stream(). Model contains Rep + IBlock(Rep) + Row + Column random effects (sigma_rep2, sigma_ib2). Computes -criterion, D-criterion, CDmean. Fully decoupled construction.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: Added optimize_famoptg(): Random Restart (RS) optimiser prep_famoptg() designs. Searches across n_restarts independent randomisations returns design best criterion value. Supports , D, , CDmean criteria. RS used exclusively p-rep constraint preserved construction every call. Added optimize_alpha_rc(): criterion-driven optimiser alpha_rc_stream() designs three search strategies: RS (Random Restart): independent randomisations, return best. SA (Simulated Annealing): iterative entry-permutation swaps temperature-governed acceptance, repeated across multiple restarts. GA (Genetic Algorithm): population entry permutations evolved via Order Crossover (OX1), swap mutation, elitism. Supports , D, , CDmean criteria. methods preserve structural constraints construction.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: evaluation optimisation functions support: -criterion: mean pairwise contrast variance (fixed effects) mean prediction error variance / PEV (random effects). Lower better. D-criterion: geometric mean contrast covariance eigenvalues (fixed effects ). Lower better. CDmean: mean coefficient determination genomic breeding value prediction, CDmean = 1 - mean_PEV / sigma_g2 (Rincent et al. 2012). Higher better. Requires random treatment effects genomic prediction model (prediction_type = "GBLUP" "PBLUP").

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: optimisers implement four-point integrity checking strategy: post-construction validation, re-check storing best, final check return, emergency fallback valid design found across iterations.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: Added alpha_rc_helpers.R: internal helper functions shared across six exported functions — sparse incidence matrix construction, AR1 precision matrices, mixed model solver, Hutchinson stochastic trace estimator (Hutchinson 1990) scalable criterion approximation large designs, Chebyshev neighbourhood enumeration, dispersion scoring function. Construction, evaluation, optimisation follow single-responsibility architecture: step separate function can called independently chained.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: evaluation optimisation functions support: "IID": independent residuals. "AR1": row-AR1 autocorrelation. "AR1xAR1": separable row × column AR1 autocorrelation.

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: design families support: Family-based adjacency scoring (cluster_source = "Family"). GRM-based clustering (cluster_source = "GRM"). Pedigree-based clustering (cluster_source = ""). Optional post-layout genetic dispersion optimisation (use_dispersion = TRUE) using swap-based local search reduce spatial clustering related entries.
